Decreased excitability during the slow increase of the power stimulation is called - useful time the rheobase
Physiological process, characterized by spreading of depolarization of cell membranes without decrement is called - accommodation

The structural and functional unit of muscle fiber is - actin myosin
Type A nerve fibers include - receptor endings preganglionic myelin fibers of the autonomic nervous system
To type B nerve fibers include - preganglionic myelin fibers of the autonomic nervous system muscle fiber
Nerve fibers of type C include - preganglionic myelin fibers of the autonomic nervous system muscle fiber
The property of smooth muscles, absent in skeletal, called - excitability conductivity
Ions play the main role in the formation of depolarization phase of AP of smooth muscle cells - sodium potessium
Muscle contraction caused by a series of super-threshold pulses with a follow-up interval exceeding the duration of a single contraction, called - complete tetanus
Muscle contraction caused by a series of super-threshold impulses entering the relaxation phase of the previous response is called - complete tetanus
Muscle contraction caused by a series of super-threshold impulses entering the contraction phase of the previous response is called - complete tetanus
Motor neuron and muscle fibers innervated by it is called - sensory-motor link neuromuscular link

Somatotropic hormone stimulates protein synthesis in cells stimulates protein catabolism

The volume of urine is regulated by pituitary hormone aldosterone somatotropin

notes.text: Anakrote of sphygmogram matches the beginning of phase exile the end of a phase of exile
The systolic pressure in the brachial artery of an adult healthy person at rest is 100-109 mm Hg 110-120 mm Hg
The difference between systolic and diastolic pressure is systolic pressure diastolic pressure
On the electrocardiogram, with an increase in the tone of the vagus nerves, it is noted ECG wave amplitude reduction increasing the duration of the QRS complex
The volume of air remaining in the lungs after a quiet exhalation is the capacity of the breath functional residual capacity
A decrease in the basic exchange rate may be a consequence of hyperfunctions of the neurohypophysis (posterior lobe) hypofunctions of the thyroid gland
The formation of primary urine in the nephron capsule is due to the reabsorption of substances in the tubules tubular secretion
What is different in composition of primary urine from blood plasma no different the lack of blood cells and protein molecules
Can the hemoglobin molecule pass through the renal filter always pass never pass
Reabsorption of chlorine ions is ensured sodium-dependent secondary transport electric gradient
Reabsorption of water is ensured osmotic gradient interaction with carriers on cell membrane
Mechanism of reabsorption of glucose and amino acids in the kidneys is provided interaction with carriers and sodium-dependent secondary active transport osmotic gradient
The formation of primary urine from blood plasma is a function proximal tubules of the nephron distal tubules
The reabsorption process is the process of passing a cellular and protein-free part of the plasma from the capillaries of the glomerulus through the barrier into the cavity of the capsule

Obligatory reabsorption of water, glucose, amino acids, urea is a function of the capillaries of the glomerulus renal corpuscle collecting ducts of the nephron distal tubules the collective tubes of the nephron
Reabsorption of glucose occurs almost entirely in the loop of Henle distal twisted rope
The process of secretion is transportation of substances from tubular urine to blood filter in the lumen of the tubules of the blood plasma
The formation of the final urine is the result filtration, reabsorption, active transportation filtration, reabsorption
Daily diuresis in norm equal 2.5-5 L 150-180 L
The value of the hydrostatic blood pressure in the glomerular capillaries is about 50 mm Hg about 40 mm Hg
The value of the oncotic pressure of the blood plasma is 50 mm Hg 20 mm Hg
A feature of the vascular bed of the nephron of the kidneys is large diameter efferent arterioles short capillaries
Obligatory reabsorption of water in kidneys is carried out in the capillaries of the glomerulus the collective tubes
Reabsorption of sodium occurs mainly in in the proximal tubule, in ascending part loop of Genle juxtaglomerular apparatus
Antidiuretic hormone increases water reabsorption in proximal tubule loop of Henle
Reabsorption of sodium and secretion of potassium in the kidneys regulates hormone thyroxine adrenaline
Where is the center of osmoregulation in the renal glomeruli in the supraoptic nucleus of the anterior hypothalamus
As a diuresis and osmolarity of urine will change at limitation of receipt of water in an organism a large volume of hypotonic urine is excreted
The mechanism of action of aldosterone on the kidneys is enhances the excretion of water enhances the reabsorption of potassium
Stabilization of acid-base state is provided by secretion in the kidneys sodium and potassium calcium and magnesium
If increase osmotic pressure of blood a large volume of hypotonic urine is excreted small volume of hypotonic urine is excreted
The gastric phase of pancreatic juice secretion is regulated gastrin secretin
